headline: PSG-Arsenal: Paris braces for a tense weekend with Champions League final and several large concerts
description: 'This is an exceptional situation that calls for an exceptional operation,' said a source within Paris's police prefecture, which is planning to deploy an additional 8,000 officers to secure the Parc des Princes football stadium and an Aya Nakamura concert.

headline: The Bolloré protégée spreading Russian propaganda in France
France heatwave sparks calls for more supervision at swimming areas after multiple drownings
US hails 'productive' talks between Israel and Lebanon
description: Friday's meeting between Israeli and Lebanese military officials was the first in decades. The Pentagon's second-in-command said on X that the 'productive military-to-military discussions' will 'inform the Department of State-led political track next week' and added that the US 'anticipates reconvening soon to continue the security track.'

headline: In Chongqing, China, the 'bangbang' porters are no longer in demand
description: These iconic workers in the vast, mountainous city have long been employed to carry goods through its steep alleyways. But modernization is making their once-essential role obsolete.

headline: 'Latte dads': The not-so-new Scandinavian fathers
description: Sweden is promoting a new masculine ideal: the caring, attentive father sipping matcha on a café terrace beside a stroller. But is this the sign of a real shift, or simply a reinvention of the 'new father' stereotype? So far, statistics have yet to confirm these parental feats.

headline: The oil crisis suits TotalEnergies' CEO perfectly
description: Between price caps at the pump and controversies over windfall profits, Patrick Pouyané is once again at the center of public debate. Despite the tensions, he dreams of proving that the multinational company serves the French people's interest.
byline: Isabelle Chaperon and  Marie de Vergès

Why German trains are never on time anymore
description: The German rail network has deteriorated over the past 20 years, to the point that Deutsche Bahn has become a source of national embarrassment.

headline: 'Reckless, racist, incompetent and treasonous': Bruce Springsteen delivers scathing indictment of Trump at Washington concert
description: During the final concert of his US tour, the 76-year-old artist accused the Republican president of 'destroying the American idea and our reputation around the world.'

headline: 38° at 11 am. French students and staff struggle with unbearable classroom temperatures
description: Across France, primary, middle and high schools have reported the severity of the situation in their overheated classrooms. Education professionals are criticizing the lack of response in recent years from both local authorities, who are responsible for school buildings, and from the Education Ministry, in charge of the safety of students and staff.
